checks.name: schema_completeness
checks.detail: All required payload fields present: structured_prompt, suggested_tools, suggested_triggers, suggested_connectors, use_case_flows, full_prompt_markdown

checks.name: flow_integrity
checks.detail: 2 flows (Error Ingestion & Classification, Cascading Failure Detection) both have connected graphs with proper start/end/decision nodes and edges

checks.name: trigger_validity
checks.detail: 4 triggers defined. 'event' trigger type is not in the SuggestedTrigger type union ('manual'|'schedule'|'polling'|'webhook'). TuneStep.tsx TRIGGER_ICONS would return undefined for 'event' type, causing potential runtime error. Two schedule triggers have valid cron. Polling trigger has cron format.

checks.name: connector_role_alignment
checks.detail: slack->chat_messaging is a valid role. Only 1 connector needed (lightweight template).

checks.name: adoption_question_fitness
checks.detail: 6 adoption_questions present and updated to standard format: slack channel, severity threshold, monitored personas, digest time, recurring threshold, retention days

checks.name: default_value_coverage
checks.detail: All adoption questions have sensible defaults. Slack connector has bot_token as only required field.

checks.name: prompt_quality
checks.detail: Identity: 73 words. Instructions: ~2500 words with 6 major sections, the most detailed of all 5 templates. Tool guidance covers event bus, execution API, Slack, and local files. 3 comprehensive examples. Thorough error handling including self-monitoring.

checks.name: safety_scan
checks.detail: 0 critical. Self-monitoring capability. Sampling mode for volume spikes. No destructive actions.

dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity 73 words with 'agent that watches the agents' framing. Instructions ~2500 words — the most detailed across all 5 templates — covering error ingestion, classification across 4 dimensions with 7 error categories, cross-workflow pattern detection (4 types), real-time alerts, daily digests, and weekly reports. Tool guidance for event bus, platform API, Slack. 3 examples with cascading failure scenario. Self-monitoring error handling.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: http_request, file_read, file_write documented. Platform event bus and execution API endpoints specified. Slack API covered. Minor: platform execution API (/api/v1/executions) is internal and may not be accessible via standard http_request.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: Conceptually excellent: event subscription for real-time errors, polling for silent failures, daily and weekly schedules for reports. However, the 'event' trigger type is not defined in the SuggestedTrigger type union, which would cause a TuneStep rendering issue. The polling trigger uses cron format rather than interval.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: Only Slack is needed as an external connector — the template is intentionally lightweight, relying on internal platform event bus and APIs. This is correct for a meta-observability agent. Minor: no connector for the platform's execution API (implicitly uses internal access).

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Exceptional state management: error_index.json (90-day rolling), error_patterns.json, persona_reliability.json with dependency maps, daily digests, weekly reports. 90-day pruning. Atomic state updates. Empty initialization on first run.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Outstanding: self-monitoring, event bus fallback to polling, deduplication, malformed event handling, Slack buffering with consolidated recovery, state file corruption handling, volume spike detection with sampling mode, performance monitoring.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: 2 flows cover the core scenarios: error ingestion with classification and cascade detection. Daily digest and weekly report flows are documented in the prompt but not as separate use_case_flows — they could be added as additional flows.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Exceptional value proposition: meta-observability for the entire persona ecosystem. Each execution produces classified errors, pattern detection, and actionable remediation suggestions. Weekly reports with reliability rankings and improvement recommendations.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: Slack API is standard. Platform event bus subscription and execution API depend on platform internals that may or may not be exposed. AI-powered error classification requires strong LLM reasoning. File-based state management is straightforward.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Highest differentiation of all 5 templates: AI-powered error classification, cascading failure detection across dependent personas, time-pattern analysis, and reliability trending. Impossible to replicate with simple automation.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: Adoption questions cover all key user-configurable values: channel, severity threshold, monitored personas, digest time, recurring threshold, retention days. All are genuinely needed.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Excellent defaults: #ops-alerts, severity=high, monitor all personas, 9AM digest, 3 occurrences threshold, 90-day retention. Could adopt with zero changes and get a working meta-monitoring agent. Only Slack bot_token requires actual setup.

issues: Trigger type 'event' is not in the SuggestedTrigger type union — TuneStep.tsx TRIGGER_ICONS lookup would return undefined, causing potential runtime error
Adoption questions had non-standard format (helpText/required/maps_to instead of context/connector_names/category) — updated to standard format
Daily digest and weekly report are documented in prompt but not as separate use_case_flows
Platform execution API (/api/v1/executions) is internal — accessibility via http_request depends on platform design

template: Normalized 6 adoption_questions from non-standard format (helpText/maps_to/required with object options) to standard format (context/connector_names/category with flat string options)
Added connector_names: ['slack'] to the slack_alert_channel question
